Considering these points, the likelihood of "Jack has a wetsuit" is elevated.     *   This means that the speaker's statement *can be true even if Tim does not have a wetsuit*, provided that Tim also *does not like swimming*. In this case, the statement becomes "If Tim likes swimming (which he doesn't), he will pack his wetsuit (which he can't)," which is a logically true "False implies False" conditional.
